sections.label: Section 1505
sections.original: ὀλώλαμεν δύʼ ὄντε , μή σφε περιίδῃς
sections.translation: do not allow them to wander poor and unwed , for they are your own kin , nor abase them to the level of my woes . Pity them , seeing them deprived of everything but you at such an age .

sections.label: Section 1510
sections.original: ξύννευσον , ὦ γενναῖε , σῇ ψαύσας χερί .
sections.translation: Promise , noble man , and touch them with your hand . To you , children , I would have given much counsel , if your minds were mature . But now pray that you may live where occasion allows , and that the life which is your lot may be happier than your father ’ s .

sections.label: Section 1515
sections.original: ἅλις ἵνʼ ἐξήκεις δακρύων · ἀλλʼ ἴθι στέγης ἔσω .
sections.translation: Your grief has had a sufficient scope : move on into the house .

sections.label: Section trochaic.1516
sections.translation: I must obey , though I do it in no way gladly .

sections.label: Section trochaic.1516a
sections.translation: Yes , for it is in season that all things are good .

sections.label: Section trochaic.1517
sections.translation: Do you know on what terms I will go ?

sections.label: Section trochaic.1517a
sections.translation: You will tell me , and then I will know when I have heard them .

sections.label: Section trochaic.1518
sections.translation: See that you send me to dwell outside this land .

sections.label: Section trochaic.1518a
sections.translation: You ask for what the god must give .

sections.label: Section trochaic.1519
sections.translation: But to the gods I have become most hateful .

sections.label: Section trochaic.1519a
sections.translation: Then you will quickly get your wish .

sections.label: Section trochaic.1520
sections.translation: So you consent ?

sections.label: Section trochaic.1520a
sections.original: ἃ μὴ φρονῶ γὰρ οὐ φιλῶ λέγειν μάτην .
sections.translation: It is not my way to say idly what I do not mean .

sections.label: Section trochaic.1521
sections.translation: Then it is time to lead me away .

sections.label: Section trochaic.1521a
sections.translation: Come , then , but let your children go .

sections.label: Section trochaic.1522
sections.translation: No , do not take them from me !

sections.label: Section trochaic.1523
sections.original: καὶ γὰρ ἁκράτησας οὔ σοι τῷ βίῳ ξυνέσπετο .
sections.translation: Do not wish to be master in all things : the mastery which you did attain has not followed you through life .

sections.label: Section trochaic.1524
sections.original: ὦ πάτρας Θήβης ἔνοικοι , λεύσσετʼ , Οἰδίπους ὅδε ,
sections.translation: Residents of our native Thebes , behold , this is Oedipus ,

sections.label: Section trochaic.1525
sections.original: ὃς τὰ κλείνʼ αἰνίγματʼ ᾔδει καὶ κράτιστος ἦν ἀνήρ ,
sections.translation: who knew the renowned riddle , and was a most mighty man . What citizen did not gaze on his fortune with envy ? See into what a stormy sea of troubles he has come ! Therefore , while our eyes wait to see the final destined day , we must call no mortal happy until

sections.label: Section trochaic.1530
sections.original: τέρμα τοῦ βίου περάσῃ μηδὲν ἀλγεινὸν παθών .
sections.translation: he has crossed life ’ s border free from pain .
